verses.commentary: All who are under the yoke of slavery addresses Christian slaves. Should consider their masters worthy of full respect commands honoring even unbelieving masters. So that God's name and our teaching may not be slandered states purpose: witness. Poor service by Christian slaves discredits the gospel.

Those who have believing masters should not show them disrespect just because they are fellow believers addresses potential abuse of equality. Instead, they should serve them even better requires superior service. Because their masters are dear to them as fellow believers grounds better service in affection. And are devoted to the welfare of their slaves assumes masters' benevolence, perhaps ideal rather than universal reality.

If anyone teaches otherwise identifies false teachers. And does not agree to the sound instruction of our Lord Jesus Christ and to godly teaching defines orthodoxy by christological and ethical standards. Sound instruction comes from Jesus himself.

They are conceited and understand nothing begins character description. They have an unhealthy interest in controversies and quarrels about words diagnoses their fixation. That result in envy, strife, malicious talk, evil suspicions catalogs the fruit: relational toxicity. Bad theology produces bad community.

And constant friction between people of corrupt mind adds ongoing conflict. Who have been robbed of the truth describes loss of sound doctrine. And who think that godliness is a means to financial gain exposes mercenary motive: using religion for profit.
But godliness with contentment is great gain offers correction. True profit is godliness plus contentment, not godliness aiming at material gain. Contentment transforms godliness into wealth.

For we brought nothing into the world, and we can take nothing out of it grounds contentment in reality. Birth and death bracket life with empty hands. Material accumulation is temporary by definition.
But if we have food and clothing, we will be content with that sets the bar low. Basic necessities suffice for contentment. Anything beyond is bonus, not requirement.

Those who want to get rich identifies the problem: desire itself, not wealth. Fall into temptation and a trap describes inevitable snare. And into many foolish and harmful desires multiplies dangers. That plunge people into ruin and destruction states the end. Wanting wealth leads to destruction, whether wealth is obtained or not.

For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil clarifies: not money but love of it. Money itself is neutral; love of it spawns evil. Some people, eager for money, have wandered from the faith shows apostasy. And pierced themselves with many griefs describes self-inflicted pain. Greed wounds the greedy.

But you, man of God contrasts Timothy with money lovers. Flee from all this commands escape. And pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance and gentleness lists six virtues to chase. Christian life is flight from vice, pursuit of virtue.

Fight the good fight of the faith militarizes faithfulness. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called commands grasping what is already possessed. When you made your good confession in the presence of many witnesses recalls Timothy's public profession, likely at baptism.

In the sight of God, who gives life to everything invokes God as witness. And of Christ Jesus, who while testifying before Pontius Pilate made the good confession adds Christ's confession as model. I charge you creates solemn obligation before divine witnesses.
To keep this command without spot or blame requires blameless obedience. Until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ sets timeframe: from now until parousia. The charge extends to Christ's return.

Which God will bring about in his own time affirms divine control of timing. God, the blessed and only Ruler piles up divine titles. The King of kings and Lord of lords asserts absolute sovereignty. No earthly power rivals divine authority.

Who alone is immortal distinguishes God from all creatures. And who lives in unapproachable light describes divine transcendence. Whom no one has seen or can see states human limitation. To him be honor and might forever. Amen erupts in doxology. Mystery and majesty unite.

Command those who are rich in this present world addresses wealthy believers. Not to be arrogant warns against pride wealth produces. Nor to put their hope in wealth, which is so uncertain, but to put their hope in God redirects trust from unstable riches to stable God. Who richly provides us with everything for our enjoyment affirms that God gives abundance for pleasure, not mere survival.
Command them to do good, to be rich in good deeds redefines wealth. And to be generous and willing to share prescribes use of material wealth. True riches are generosity, not accumulation.

In this way they will lay up treasure for themselves as a firm foundation for the coming age echoes Jesus' teaching on storing treasure in heaven. So that they may take hold of the life that is truly life defines authentic existence. Present generosity secures future reality. Real life is found through giving, not grasping.

Timothy, guard what has been entrusted to your care commands stewardship of gospel truth. Turn away from godless chatter and the opposing ideas of what is falsely called knowledge warns against false teaching masquerading as wisdom. The label knowledge does not guarantee truth.

Which some have professed and in so doing have departed from the faith reports actual apostasy. False knowledge led to faith abandonment. Grace be with you all closes with blessing. Letter framed by grace: greeting and farewell.
